headline: Lilly's Foundayo weight loss pill clears heart safety bar in largest diabetes trial yet
summary: Eli Lilly reported Phase 3 results showing cardiovascular safety for its oral GLP-1 Foundayo as the company prepares a Type 2 diabetes submission. The ACHIEVE-4 trial enrolled 2,749 participants across 15 countries, comparing Foundayo with insulin glargine and meeting non-inferiority for major cardiovascular events with a hazard ratio of 0.84. After 52 weeks, A1C dropped 1.6 percentage points on Foundayo versus 1.0 with insulin, and weight fell by 17.9 pounds (8.8%) while insulin users gained about 3.1 pounds. Safety aligned with GLP-1 class effects; 10.6% discontinued due to gastrointestinal side effects, and no liver safety signal emerged. A secondary analysis noted a 57% reduction in all-cause mortality, not adjusted for multiple comparisons, and Lilly plans an FDA filing by the end of Q2.

raw.headline: Lilly's Foundayo weight loss pill clears heart safety bar in largest diabetes trial yet
raw.summary: Eli Lilly reported Phase 3 results showing cardiovascular safety for its oral GLP-1 Foundayo as the company prepares a Type 2 diabetes submission. After 52 weeks, A1C dropped 1.6 percentage points on Foundayo versus 1.0 with insulin, and weight fell by 17.9 pounds (8.8%) while insulin users gained about 3.1 pounds. Safety aligned with GLP-1 class effects; 10.6% discontinued due to gastrointestinal side effects, and no liver safety signal emerged. A secondary analysis noted a 57% reduction in all-cause mortality, not adjusted for multiple comparisons, and Lilly plans an FDA filing by the end of Q2.
